mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces near-term discount rates and supports risk assets, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days by improving growth expectations more than it lifts safe-haven demand.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day yield changes are negative in more than 60% of low-FEDFUNDS observations or if the sign flips after controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising faster than the prior 3-month average, inflation repricing lifts expected future policy rates and term premia, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if upward CPIAUCSL crossings are followed by negative 20-day yield changes more than half the time, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL growth is below its 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weaker labor demand and softer growth, which reduces expected inflation and policy tightening, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day yield changes are positive in more than 55% of triggered unemployment rises or if the signal vanishes when PAYEMS growth is also positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand strength; that combination pushes nominal growth expectations higher and lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if either RSAFS or HOUST alone explains the sign and the joint effect adds no incremental predictive power, or if the pair is not followed by positive 20-day yield changes more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO with stable or lower CPIAUCSL lowers supply-side inflation pressure and reduces the need for higher long rates, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL 3-month change is below zero or flat
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO moves with subdued CPIAUCSL are followed by positive yield changes more often than negative ones, or if the joint condition does not outperform INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in PAYEMS signals labor-market resilience and stronger near-term income growth, which raises expected nominal demand and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds its prior 6-month average by 1 standard deviation
failure_condition: Reject if payroll acceleration events are followed by negative 5-day yield changes in a majority of cases or if the effect only appears when UNRATE is falling.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, a rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS marks an unambiguous growth downturn, and that pairing drives the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE stays above 7.0% and PAYEMS trends lower for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if the high-UNRATE, falling-PAYEMS regime does not produce negative 60-day yield changes in at least 60% of occurrences or if the interaction adds no power beyond UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO together with rising GDPC1 signals real activity normalization rather than inflation panic, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations recover.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous improvements in INDPRO and GDPC1 are followed by flat or negative 60-day yield changes more than 55% of the time, or if the sign reverses when CPIAUCSL is accelerating.
